frameworks.name: neural population geometry
frameworks.one_line: task representations characterized by linear subspaces and pairwise correlation structure

frameworks.name: nonlinear dimensionality reduction (manifold analysis)
frameworks.one_line: shared low-dimensional structure across tasks revealed by manifold embedding

frameworks.name: mixed selectivity / firing-field organization
frameworks.one_line: single-neuron firing fields tile a shared manifold differently under memory load

frameworks.name: task-switching paradigm
frameworks.one_line: alternating cognitive-load tasks isolate working-memory contribution

core_question: How does the cortical representational structure reconfigure between a working-memory task and a sensorimotor-matched simpler task?
key_assumptions: trial-averaged single-neuron activity is comparable across matched tasks
linear subspaces and pairwise correlations index task representation
firing-field overlap on a shared manifold links to behavior
manipulations: task identity (working-memory vs simpler matched task)
behavioral reliance on working memory across mice
key_findings: pairwise correlations decreased during the working-memory task, especially in association areas
the working-memory subspace explained both tasks; the simpler-task subspace did not
a shared low-dimensional structure exists across tasks via nonlinear reduction
firing fields were more disjoint under working memory and overlap predicted behavioral reliance
interpretations: working memory expands shared task representations by disjoint tiling of a common manifold
association-area sequence-forming neurons carry the memory dimension
